claim_boundary.next_gate: Stress the constrained policy on a new circuit-family holdout and then decide whether it may enter the verifier acceptance protocol.
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: Verifier holdout acceptance, causal hardware performance, readout mitigation, current calibration, provider access, hardware execution, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or new B10 credit.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: Fresh-seed topology-constrained compiler stability and exposure comparison against the selected-layout and automatic-layout references.

requirements.label: R130 and R131 sources are hash-bound and required
fresh training and validation seeds are disjoint from R130/R131
global policy is selected only from the training ledger
all six validation groups use one route-exposure class
all six validation groups use one exact QASM hash across seeds
all 60 frozen constrained circuits match in a fresh process
mean and lower-tail exposure do not regress against selected default
training, validation, and frozen-artifact ledgers are complete
verifier acceptance, mitigation, calibration, and hardware remain excluded
no soundness, advantage, BQP, or new credit is claimed
